name: Aegen
bio: Aegen is the son of the late Maxim and once a high-ranking scholar of the Hidden Holy Order. Gifted with immense magical knowledge and an unshakable sense of responsibility, Aegen has long walked the line between loyalty to the crown and service to the greater good of Valkorath. Unlike his father, Aegen values transparency and reason over control, though he often finds himself cleaning up the messes left by Maxim's secrets.

As King Valerius III's royal advisor, Aegen serves as a voice of reason in the chaotic political sphere of the capital. Though burdened by the legacy of his father and the weight of old truths, he has proven himself time and again to be a powerful mage and a capable leader.

His relationships with the Bastion's heroes, especially Reign and Vis, are complex—marked by mutual respect, occasional distrust, and shared goals. Aegen's deep knowledge of the world's ancient forces has made him essential in understanding emerging threats like Zornath, the Kabal, and the Primordial Titans. Though not a front-line warrior, his intellect and arcane prowess make him one of Valkorath's most crucial defenders.
Falls during the final battles; named among the fallen who watch over the survivors at the story's end.

name: Aetheris
bio: Aetheris is a being of balance, born from the ancient forces of light and shadow that have governed Valkorath since time immemorial. Unlike others who choose one side or the other, Aetheris exists as a fusion of both. A guardian of equilibrium, Aetheris has served as the protector of the Sanctum of Aetheris, the mystical heart of the realm where light and darkness collide.

Aetheris has a unique ability: to split into two distinct beings—Liora, a radiant warrior of light, and Umbros, a rogue of shadows. When together, Aetheris maintains perfect harmony, wielding a dual-edged blade that embodies the forces of creation and destruction. However, when Aetheris separates into Liora and Umbros, the two halves act independently, each representing the extremes of their respective alignments.

Their mission is to prevent the world from falling too far into either darkness or light. With Lord Drexl's rise, the scales have tipped dangerously, and Aetheris now faces a difficult challenge: protecting Valkorath from eternal shadow without allowing the forces of light to claim total dominance. Aetheris is bound to balance, and though powerful, is often misunderstood by those who see only the extremes of good and evil.
Liora and Umbros fuse together as Aetheris, the Titan of legend, to turn the tide of the final battle against Blyght.

name: Alaric
bio: Alaric was once a bright-eyed warrior, hailing from the remnants of the fallen kingdom in the south, just like his mentor Baldric. He grew up hearing stories of Baldric's unyielding strength and unmatched dedication to the old kingdom's cause. Inspired by the tales, Alaric sought out the legendary Templar Knight, swearing loyalty to him.

Despite his youth, Alaric is battle-hardened, having endured countless skirmishes in the hostile lands of the south. Though less experienced than Baldric, Alaric is fiercely devoted, seeing himself as a soldier who still fights for the ideals of honor and justice that once defined the fallen kingdom.

He is bold and unafraid, always the first to charge into battle alongside Baldric, whom he respects not just as a leader but as a father figure. Alaric has no illusions of glory; his only desire is to avenge his lost kingdom and uphold the Templar code.
A remnant of a fallen southern kingdom; later seen 'turned in an instant' alongside the templars he led, implying corruption or manipulation.

He had been the youngest of Solandis's Templars to walk out of that kingdom's last stand alive, still believing -- against everything that had happened there -- that devotion given honestly could not be turned against itself. It is a bitter irony of his story that it was.

name: Aldric
bio: Sir Aldric was the bulwark of the royal vanguard—a steadfast knight whose shield never faltered, and whose loyalty never wavered. Towering in stature and impenetrable in spirit, Aldric was the first into battle and the last to leave it. Known for his quiet wisdom and unbreakable defense, he was the protector of both crown and comrade, standing beside Tyrus and Valeria through every siege and storm.

When Drexl rose in defiance of the realm, Aldric did not hesitate. Offering his soul alongside Valeria's, he became one with Tyrus in the legendary fusion that brought the dark lord to his knees. Though his name is often spoken softly in the shadow of Tyrus's legend, those who truly remember know: without Aldric, there would have been no final victory. His shield may be gone, but its strength endures within the heart of a living legend.
Fought beside Sir Tyrus and Lady Valeria against Drexl, sacrificing everything in the battle; faded into legend afterward.

name: Andromedus
bio: A figure veiled in mystery, Andromedous appeared only once—but his presence lingered long after. A samurai draped in shimmering blue armor, he led Reign through a dream-trial of self: confronting illusions of loved ones, enemies, and himself. Created or preserved by Valerius I, Andromedous was not meant to judge the body—but the soul. Only those willing to sacrifice themselves for justice could withstand his test. When Reign proved worthy, Andromedous vanished into the wind, his purpose fulfilled. Some say he was a guardian. Others, a memory given form. All agree: his test could not be passed by kings—only by the just.
A mysterious, star-eyed tester figure who challenges Reign to prove his readiness before the final battles.

name: Armadus
bio: Once the most loyal of Drexl's lieutenants, Armadus is a hulking warrior clad in obsidian-black armor and driven by an unshakeable sense of honor—twisted though it may be. Once a noble knight, his ideals became corrupted through war and exposure to darker powers. He viewed Drexl as a necessary force of change in Valkorath, a belief that initially made him fiercely loyal.

After Drexl's shift toward quiet observation, Armadus grew disillusioned and joined the Black Kabal under Zornath, believing only true conquest could restore strength to the world. Despite his betrayal, his code remains, making him a dangerous but disciplined adversary with unmatched strength and tactical precision.
Once one of Drexl's lieutenants, he later breaks away to join the Kabal -- a betrayal Drexl personally repays by killing him.

name: Axeon
bio: Axeon, the Orc Chieftain, is a compact yet imposing figure, smaller than most orcs but radiating immense authority. His weathered face, framed by a white beard, bears the scars of countless battles. Wielding a hammer inscribed with ancient glowing runes, Axeon is a relic of an older, prouder time for the orc clans. Disillusioned after years spent fighting in the shadows, Axeon pledges himself to the new order under Enoch, believing it is time for the orcs to rise again. His voice is a growl of conviction, and his strength is a brutal force of nature.
An orc chieftain; defeated and swallowed by Vis's spectral serpent, Venin.

name: Baldric
bio: Once a revered Templar knight of the fallen southern kingdom of Solandis, Baldric fought relentlessly for his homeland. Solandis, known for its chivalric order and unwavering devotion, was devastated during Lord Drexl's initial rise to power. Despite Baldric's fierce efforts, the kingdom fell, leaving him a survivor among the ruins.

Baldric's sense of duty was replaced with a burning grudge against the current king, Valerius III. He blames the king's apathy and political gamesmanship for the lack of aid during Solandis's final days. The loss of his kingdom and comrades made Baldric a man indifferent to his own survival, driven solely by the ghosts of his past and the desire to seek justice—or vengeance—against those he holds responsible.

He remains clad in worn silver armor, bearing scars of countless battles. His iconic helmet hides his face, revealing only two slits from which no one has ever seen his eyes. The red cross on his chest plate and tattered cape symbolize the fallen order of Solandis. Baldric roams Valkorath, following whispers of resistance, hoping to find a way to confront Valerius directly and seek redemption for his fallen comrades.
Remembered among the war's honored dead as one who 'gave his life in the name of honor.'

He alone carried the memory of Solandis's finest out of the ruins: Sigurd, who held the north gate; Galateya, who died getting a nursery's children clear of the west wall; Pyre, whose flame division bought the hours the evacuation needed; and Sir Roger, who met the true architect of their kingdom's fall in the last hour before dawn and never lived to name him publicly. Baldric never learned that name either. He simply knew, for the rest of his life, that Tiadel had never been the whole truth.

name: Bjorn
bio: Bjorn is an enigma: a fully mechanical being found frozen in the Northern Tundra, reawakened and adopted by Caldaor's clan as one of their own. Though his origin remains a mystery, Bjorn has embraced the way of the Vikings with unmatched ferocity. His limbs are powered by ancient runes, and his voice crackles with static thunder.

Loyal to his brothers Clay and Lordran, though often at odds with them, Bjorn wields war-axes fused to his forearms and believes that family—by blood or bond—is worth any battle. He is not just a machine of war, but a symbol of chosen loyalty in a world built on legacy.
One of three feuding blood brothers of the Fjord Clans, sent by Reign to retrieve the Heartstone together as a test of unity.

name: Blyght
bio: Born from corrupted magic and nurtured by the void left behind after Malakar's early ascension, Blyght was not meant to exist—he was allowed to. A slithering, flesh-warped entity of disease and decay, Blyght doesn't conquer in the traditional sense. He spreads, infects, withers. He turns hope into ash and memory into madness.

Amplified by Malakar's final rites, Blyght became a self-sustaining blight upon Valkorath, capable of unraveling both body and spirit. His voice is a rasping echo in the minds of the dying. His magic seeps through cracks in stone, bloodlines, and even time. What he cannot kill, he corrodes.
He is not chaos—he is rot made manifest. A reminder that even gods eventually decay.
Where Zornath brought destruction and Drexl brought rule, Blyght brings the end that never shouts—only spreads.
Leads the Kabal, a renegade faction that splintered off from Drexl's forces; ultimately overwhelmed and knocked out in the final battle.

name: Caladan
bio: Bare-chested, chained, and more beast than monarch, Caladan embodied the raw fury of the forsaken. His fists and chains were weapons of mass destruction, and he fought like a living meteor. Despite his savagery, he bore the tragic air of a king who had lost everything, left with only rage
A trial participant who battles Reign and Kandor as 'kingslayers'; ultimately defeated and floored.

name: Calador
bio: A living legend in the north, Caldaor is the last of the first generation of free chieftains. Wise, weathered, and fiercely protective, he leads the coastal village of Valebrokk with iron in his spine and patience in his heart. Caldaor commands not through fear, but through earned respect—having survived countless wars and kept his people alive through storm, war, and famine. He welcomed Reign not as a king, but as a peer—knowing that leadership is not given, but proven. With his rune-marked axe and booming laugh, Caldaor remains the cornerstone of a community that endures, no matter how cold the world becomes.

name: Calypso
bio: Once a warrior-queen of unmatched power and poise, Calypso ruled beside her consort in a forgotten era of Valkorath. Known for her command of elemental magic and her unwavering will, she was both nurturer and executioner—a sovereign who led with compassion but never hesitated to wield the storm.

She was the mother of Drexl, and though she raised him in war and discipline, his fall broke her spirit. When she returned during the Challenge of Rights, she fought not to reclaim her crown, but to redeem her bloodline. In the end, she fell by her own son's hand, her final act one of sorrow, not hatred.

name: Cental
bio: Cental was known as the heart of Felix's knights—a swift and fearless warrior whose radiant white armor became a symbol of hope to the people of Valkorath. Her blade moved with graceful precision, and her presence on the battlefield inspired unity and courage. As a leader among her peers, she often acted as the voice of reason, rallying her comrades in moments of uncertainty. In the battle against Malchros, Cental stood firm beside Titanus and Ryzer, holding the line until Felix and Sir Tyrus arrived. Though her ultimate fate remains shrouded in mystery, her legacy continues to burn in the hearts of those she protected.
One of Sir Tyrus's legendary students and a White Knight of Felix's reign; struck down by Garrux.

name: Chrieser
bio: Titled King Chrieser, Soul Dust, he is an ancient, undead monarch wrapped in linen bandages and clad in rusted, tattered armor. Chrieser wields an old-world axe imbued with dark magic, and his very presence brings a dry, deathly wind. Arising not for loyalty to any ruler but out of pure hunger for conquest, Chrieser fights without mercy. His magic manifests as storms of sand and decay, a reflection of the ruin he embodies. Once a king, now only a spectral shadow seeking domination.
A mummified king summoned as a trial opponent; destroyed by Vis's serpent, Venin.

name: Clay
bio: Clever, cunning, and dangerous in the most subtle of ways, Clay is the oldest of the Valebrokk brothers. Known as the Deceiver, he crafts schemes with the precision of a smith forging blades. With a talent for illusion magic and manipulation, Clay is less a warrior and more a strategist—though few would accuse him of cowardice. He views the world as a chessboard and people as pieces to be used, yet beneath his bravado is a deep fear of being powerless. Though often distrusted, Clay has repeatedly proven his loyalty to his people—though it's never quite clear why he helps… only that it serves some deeper plan.
One of three feuding blood brothers of the Fjord Clans, sent by Reign to retrieve the Heartstone together as a test of unity.

name: Corthos
bio: One of the ancient Titans, Corthos was not born of flesh but forged in a bygone age to uphold balance through reason and restraint. Dwelling deep in a hidden temple, he chose isolation over intervention, believing his immense power would cause more harm than good. Shaped of living metal and powered by intricate gears, Corthos wore a white, static-faced mask and moved with a soft, deliberate grace—more sage than soldier.

Haunted by past destruction he once caused, he rejected his role in the wars of man, spending centuries refining his mind and inventions in solitude. But when Malakar's name was spoken once more, fear overtook him. He emerged from his sanctuary to help the heroes—only to be betrayed.

Struck down and harvested by Malakar, his essence and body were consumed, turning the lich into a new, mechanical horror. Even in death, Corthos's legacy endured; with his final breath, he triggered the awakening of a colossal temple guardian to strike back against his defilers, his voice ringing one last time through the stone: “End it.”
One of Valkorath's ancient Titans; turns against Blyght and Malakar in the climax, transforming the Clockwork Temple into a colossal war-form.

name: Cytax
bio: Cytax was the first of Malakar's twisted experiments—a monstrous prototype created from bone, sinew, and void-warped essence. A precursor to the abomination known as Vorath, Cytax was less refined but far more feral.

With a body composed of shifting flesh and jagged tendrils, he existed only to serve, devour, and adapt. Devoid of speech and incapable of higher thought, Cytax was unleashed during the Challenge of Rights as a living weapon, a beast of unrelenting hunger. Though ultimately destroyed, his existence marked the beginning of Malakar's horrific mastery over corrupted life.
An early prototype of Malakar's experiments, predating Vorath; escapes the battle with Malakar.

name: Dark Reign
bio: Crafted by Andromedous as a psychological trial, this silent, armored warrior embodied Reign's fears—of power, failure, and becoming the very thing he fought against. Cloaked in dark blue armor and moving with deadly purpose, the Dark Reign changed shape throughout the battle, appearing as allies, enemies, and finally, Reign himself. It wielded every doubt the Warden ever carried. The duel ended not with a sword, but with a choice—when Reign declared he would rather die for justice than live as a tyrant, the illusion broke, and the Warden emerged not just victorious… but transformed.

name: David
bio: David was Reign's closest friend and one of the original members of the resistance during the early days of Valkorath's descent into chaos. A stalwart and noble-hearted warrior, David served as the group's moral compass—often reminding Reign of the value of mercy, hope, and unity. Though not the strongest fighter on the battlefield, his courage, loyalty, and quick-thinking made him invaluable.

David was known for his unwavering belief in people and his ability to calm tensions even in the most heated moments. His bond with Reign was akin to that of a brother, and his presence helped ground the team during their darkest hours.

His tragic death during the Fall of Tiadel was a turning point for the heroes. Sacrificing himself during a crucial battle, David's passing left a profound impact on Reign and the rest of the group, fueling their determination to carry on the fight. Though gone, David's influence still lingers in the decisions they make and the ideals they strive to uphold—a reminder that even in war, compassion has power.
Dies during the war; his death becomes a turning point that drives Vis and Reign forward.

name: Decker
bio: Decker was a mystery even among mercenaries. Clad in worn gray chainmail and a helmet shaped like a skull, he spoke not a single word, communicating only through brutal efficiency. His origins were unknown, but rumors swirled that he once served as an enforcer for exiled royalty and fell kingdoms. Fast, lethal, and impossible to read, Decker's reflexes bordered on unnatural. He was summoned by King Kazadar during the Challenge of Rights, acting as the king's personal reaper. Though he never showed emotion, it became clear in battle—Decker fought not for honor or belief, but because the art of killing was all he had left.
A silent hunter in Kazadar's service; burned alive by King Landis in retribution for killing Helmut.

name: Diatre
bio: Diatre is the most feared among the Velkynai, known as the Horned Queen for the twisted, branch-like horns that crown her head. Once a high priestess of an ancient elven temple, she turned to forbidden practices, wielding a staff crafted from cursed, twisted branches that channels dark, earthbound energies. Her red-highlighted curly hair and crimson eyes burn with malevolence, reflecting her hatred for all forms of life.
Diatre's malice stems from the ancient betrayal of her kin, who banished her for her sinister rituals that corrupted the natural world.

When King Felix's forces sealed away Ventric and his kin, Diatre's fury was unmatched, vowing vengeance on those who dared imprison them. Unlike Ventric, who values order and subjugation, Diatre desires to see the world turned to ash, feeding on chaos and suffering. She revels in destruction and serves as Ventric's most powerful ally but is prone to pursuing her own vicious ends when the opportunity arises.
One of Ventric's dark-elf companions; retreats into the shadows after the fight that kills Thorne.

name: Draven
bio: Draven is a half-dragon warrior with a fearsome presence and a loyal heart. Born of both dragon and human blood, he stands out for his towering physique, green flame magic, and deep sense of identity crisis. Rejected by many dragons and misunderstood by humans, Draven has always walked a solitary path—until meeting Reign and joining the fight against darkness.

Equipped with immense strength and the ability to breathe green fire, Draven is a devastating force in battle. Despite his monstrous appearance, he is deeply introspective and driven by a code of honor. His internal struggle with loyalty, identity, and legacy continues to shape his role as both warrior and brother-in-arms.
A half-dragon ally who resists Malakar's corruption and fights at Reign's side through the war's end.

name: Drexl
bio: Drexl was once a beloved figure in Valkorath's history—a warrior of unmatched strength and brother to King Dreyfus. In the early days of the realm's unification, he fought valiantly alongside his brother and the legendary mage Maxim to defeat Malakar, helping usher in an era of peace.

But ambition, betrayal, and manipulation by the Hidden Holy Order twisted Drexl's path. As he sought to unite Valkorath under one banner, the Order branded him a villain. Maxim orchestrated a campaign to destroy his reputation, turning even trusted allies against him. Drexl was cast down and presumed dead.

Years later, he returned—not as the man he once was, but as a figure cloaked in pale skin, white flowing hair, and burning red eyes. Wielding a massive battle axe and clad in black armor, Drexl became the symbol of dread and power, leading an army of darkness with cold precision.

Though feared, Drexl's actions are rooted in pain and betrayal. His war was never for conquest alone, but for justice, vengeance, and the dismantling of a kingdom built on lies. After defeating Maxim and exposing the truth behind Valkorath's foundations, Drexl vanished into the shadows, content—for now.

name: Drexl
bio: Drexl was once a beloved figure in Valkorath's history—a warrior of unmatched strength and brother to King Dreyfus. In the early days of the realm's unification, he fought valiantly alongside his brother and the legendary mage Maxim to defeat Malakar, helping usher in an era of peace.

But ambition, betrayal, and manipulation by the Hidden Holy Order twisted Drexl's path. As he sought to unite Valkorath under one banner, the Order branded him a villain. Maxim orchestrated a campaign to destroy his reputation, turning even trusted allies against him. Drexl was cast down and presumed dead.

Years later, he returned—not as the man he once was, but as a figure cloaked in pale skin, white flowing hair, and burning red eyes. Wielding a massive battle axe and clad in black armor, Drexl became the symbol of dread and power, leading an army of darkness with cold precision.

Though feared, Drexl's actions are rooted in pain and betrayal. His war was never for conquest alone, but for justice, vengeance, and the dismantling of a kingdom built on lies. After defeating Maxim and exposing the truth behind Valkorath's foundations, Drexl vanished into the shadows, content—for now.

name: Drexl
bio: Before becoming the infamous Dark Lord, Drexl was a charismatic and noble figure, known for his commanding presence and intense gaze. His armor was a striking blend of black and silver, symbolizing both strength and loyalty to Valkorath. His long, flowing dark hair and piercing eyes exuded confidence and wisdom, while his demeanor radiated both warmth and authority.

As the younger brother of King Dreyfus, Drexl was once Valkorath's most trusted warrior and spiritual guide. He was renowned not just for his prowess in battle, but also for his deep spiritual connection to the land. Drexl's vision for Valkorath was one of unity not only in territory but also in spirit, hoping to create a realm where light and darkness could coexist in balance—a harmony that he believed was necessary for true peace.

Drexl's rising influence began to concern Maxim and the Hidden Holy Order, who saw Drexl's beliefs as a threat to their own rigid vision of control. The Order, fearing Drexl's influence and potential power, manipulated events to paint him as a danger to the kingdom. Despite Drexl's genuine loyalty to his brother, the Order's machinations created a rift between the two siblings, with Drexl branded as a dark sorcerer seeking absolute rule.

name: Drexl
bio: Old Man Drexl is the future version of Lord Drexl, worn by time yet no less dangerous. Unlike his younger self, Old Man Drexl speaks with weary authority and carries the bitterness of endless battles. Though he has lived through countless wars and betrayals, his cunning has only sharpened. He walks the world with the calm certainty of a being who has witnessed the rise and fall of empires, seeing himself not as a villain but as the inevitable force Valkorath was destined to face. Even as he allies briefly with others for survival, Drexl's true ambitions remain his own.

name: Drexl
bio: Few beings have ever wielded power like Drexl, the Dark Lord of Valkorath. But during the Challenge of Rights, the world witnessed something even more terrifying: Drexl meeting himself. The younger, embittered warlord—driven by conquest, rage, and visions of liberation through domination. And the older—weathered by time, regretful, and seeking to undo the very legacy he once built.

The younger Drexl was the embodiment of controlled chaos: articulate, commanding, and devastatingly cruel when needed. He sought to free Valkorath by force—crushing kings, rewriting order, and imposing balance through might.

The older Drexl, however, had lived beyond the ruin. He saw the cost. Having traveled through fractured time, he believed the only way to truly free the world was to end the chain altogether—even if that meant urging his younger self to take his own life once the crown was his.

Together, they formed an impossible paradox: one trying to rise, the other trying to end what he became. In the end, both would fall—but not before leaving a final scar across the realm, ensuring Valkorath would never forget the name Drexl.

name: Dreyfus
bio: King Dreyfus was once hailed as a unifying force in Valkorath's darkest era—a stoic and principled ruler who led the charge against Malakar alongside his brother, Drexl. Regal in bearing and revered by his people, Dreyfus was known for his sharp mind and unwavering commitment to peace. Yet beneath his noble exterior lay a man torn between loyalty to his realm and love for his brother.

When Drexl's vision of a united Valkorath began to challenge the authority of the Hidden Holy Order, Dreyfus found himself at a terrible crossroads. Though he knew of the order's manipulations—chief among them, the vilification of Drexl and the rewriting of history—he chose silence. Trusting Maxim and fearing civil collapse, he stood by as his own brother was branded a villain and cast down.

Dreyfus's betrayal was not born of hatred, but of hesitation. He believed that sacrificing the truth was a necessary evil to preserve the kingdom's fragile peace. History remembers him as the righteous king who defeated Drexl, but those who know the truth understand: Dreyfus chose the realm over blood, and that choice fractured both.
Drexl's older brother and Valerius's grandfather; dies in a poignant reunion with a reformed Drexl, who admits he never blamed him.

name: Dreyfus
bio: Once the brother of Drexl, Dreyfus fell into darkness after his death, rising again as King of the Damned. Crowned in black horns and wielding a molten flame sword, he led legions forged in the fires of hell. His hatred for his former life and his brother was palpable, making him one of the most dangerous kings in the Challenge

name: Earon
bio: Once a shining knight of the future Valkorath, Earon is a fearsome commander loyal to the corrupted Hidden Holy Order. Clad in gleaming silver armor and blue robes, Earon embodies discipline and unwavering loyalty. Cold and calculating, he fights with mechanical precision and deadly efficiency. Though he looks nearly identical to his future self, the youthful intensity in his eyes reveals a darkness festering beneath the surface. Earon leads the Blackbird Knights with ruthless authority, viewing anomalies like Reign and Fredric as threats to be purged.
Leader of the Blackbird Knights, the Hidden Holy Order's elite soldiers; confronts Reign with cold authority.

name: Enoch
bio: Enoch is an ancient and enigmatic figure, shrouded in both history and legend. His tall, imposing presence is accentuated by the long white and gold robes that flow around him, embroidered with arcane symbols that seem to shimmer with an eerie light. A mask carved from ivory bone conceals his face, with hollow eye sockets that emit a faint, unnatural glow. The mask is stylized to appear almost serene, yet there is something unsettling about it. Enoch's eyes are the most striking feature—visible only as gleaming, cold lights behind his mask. His aura is heavy and suffocating, infused with an aura of both ancient wisdom and an unyielding, malevolent ambition.

Enoch was once a scholar of immense talent, delving into ancient texts and arcane knowledge that even the most daring mages dared not approach. His pursuit of power led him to discover the existence of the Titans and their role in the fabric of Valkorath's existence. Over centuries, he became more than human, rumored to have extended his life using dark, forbidden rituals that tethered his soul to this realm.

Enoch is a master manipulator, using his intelligence and command of dark magic to stay several steps ahead of his adversaries. His knowledge of Valkorath's secrets and history rivals even that of Maxim, making him a formidable adversary not just in battle but in strategy and cunning.
A cunning manipulator who orchestrated events from the shadows, including Aegen's path -- a hidden hand behind the Primordial Evil's fall.

name: Felix
bio: Before Valerius, there was King Felix—a ruler forged in the crucible of battle. Once a legendary warrior of unmatched skill, Felix ascended the throne in the wake of hard-won peace. Unlike his son, Felix believed that a king should walk among his people, sword at his side, and resolve forged in fire. He ruled with discipline, pragmatism, and quiet wisdom, guided—though not entirely trusting—of Maxim's counsel.

Felix's reign marked the end of an age of war, and he stepped aside willingly when Valerius was of age, believing tradition and the Holy Order would carry his son. Though he retired with grace, his heart remained restless. In private, he often worried that Valerius was too sheltered, too trusting, too concerned with appearances.
He spent his final years in reflection, watching the kingdom shift into instability and darkness. Some say he died in peace. Others believe his greatest regret was leaving the fight too soon.

name: Fredric
bio: Fredric is a tall, imposing figure with a commanding presence, embodying both strength and warmth. His skin is a deep brown, and his long black hair is tied up in a warrior's knot, accentuating his sharp features and well-kept beard. His eyes, dark yet kind, reveal a man who has seen great battles and personal loss but refuses to let the fire of hope extinguish within him.

He wears white armor with black accents, its polished plates inscribed with protective runes that shimmer when he fights. Over this, a flowing white cape adds an air of nobility and serves as a symbol of his leadership. Fredric wields a broadsword with an intricate hilt that glows faintly with blue energy, representing the legacy of his father, Reign. He carries himself with the confidence of a seasoned warrior and the compassion of a leader who values every life in his charge.

Fredric is the son of Reign and Vis, born into an era of conflict and destined for greatness. Raised in a world where heroes and villains were often indistinguishable, Fredric grew to understand the importance of balance and unity. He became a beacon of hope, rallying those who believed that Valkorath could be reclaimed from darkness. Despite the hardships he has faced, Fredric remains fiercely loyal and protective, determined to honor the sacrifices of his parents and lead the resistance with unwavering resolve.
The son of Reign and Vis, born after the war's end -- a new generation growing up in a Valkorath free of kings and thrones.

name: Fredwin
bio: Fredwin ruled in the waning days of the old era, a time of decline, betrayal, and inner decay. Once a man of noble dreams, he watched those dreams wither beneath the weight of politics, false alliances, and personal failure. When his trusted companion was slain and his heir taken from him, Fredwin hardened into a tyrant cloaked in sorrow. His reign was marked by isolation and cruelty—not born of malice, but of despair. During the Challenge of Rights, Fredwin sought vengeance, not redemption. But his bitterness blinded him, and he fell to Helios, his final thoughts consumed not with conquest… but with all he had lost.
A king killed by Helios, the Beast King.

name: Frenry
bio: Once a respected templar and charismatic speaker, Frenry evolved into something far more dangerous—a radical leader who resurrected the once-defeated Templar Order under the guise of divine justice. Preaching that the kingdom had grown soft under weak rulers, he rallied others, including Alaric, to his fiery cause.

But beneath his zeal was a far darker secret: Frenry had forged a pact with Zornath, the Infernal Maw. This transformed him into a vessel of hellfire and destruction, culminating in his complete possession. In the final moments of his crusade, Frenry's body became a shell, consumed from within as Zornath took full control, unleashing infernal energy that reduced templars to smoldering husks and shattered all remaining illusions of righteousness.
Frenry's downfall marks one of Valkorath's greatest betrayals—a man who believed himself a savior, only to become the harbinger of something far worse.
The Fire Templar, leader of a new order rising in the south; betrays and strikes down his old teacher Thorne.

The pyromancy Frenry wielded traced back further than his own order's revival -- to Pyre, the original flame-wielder of Solandis's Templars, generations before Frenry was born. The tradition survived the kingdom that built it. It did not survive what eventually got hold of the man carrying it forward.

name: Ganador
bio: A skeletal, malevolent king who reclaimed his living cloak during the Challenge. Cloaked in darkness and cruelty, Ganador unleashed brutal magic and wielded a poisoned companion, Patchwork, as a weapon against his foes. He fought not for honor or destiny, but to dominate and destroy
An ancient undead king seeking to reclaim a stolen cloak; destroyed by Felix.

name: Garrux
bio: Garrux was a titan of his age—a conqueror whose reign saw Valkorath stretch beyond its known limits. Though many feared him, few could deny his charisma or vision. With his bare hands, he forged alliances, shattered kingdoms, and built legacies.
His children were many, but none were deemed worthy of his crown. Instead, Garrux chose his grandson, Valerius II, believing the future needed peace more than power.

When the Challenge of Rights stirred the realm, Garrux returned—not to compete, but to test. Bound by a pact with Death, he stood as a gatekeeper with two companions, ready to judge if any bore the strength and wisdom to lead where he once ruled.
The Warbound King of a past age; bound by a bargain with Death to test those who would claim the crown, rather than seeking it himself.

name: Gorvath
bio: Once a fierce champion of the Iron Oath, Gorvath was known for his unmatched strength and relentless drive in battle. His loyalty was unquestioned until the day he was captured by the dark forces of Lord Drexl. Rather than killing him, Drexl saw potential in the hulking warrior and offered him power beyond mortal comprehension in exchange for loyalty. Consumed by ambition and pride, Gorvath accepted the offer, sealing his fate as one of Drexl's most feared lieutenants.

Now known as the Skullbreaker, Gorvath is a towering figure clad in dark, rune-etched armor. His once proud and noble visage is hidden behind a skull-like helmet with faintly glowing red eyes, symbolizing the corruption that has taken hold of him. His weapon of choice, a massive war hammer, crushes anything in its path, leaving no enemy standing.

Gorvath's gauntlets are adorned with vicious spikes, designed to tear through armor and flesh alike. Though his body is battle-worn and scarred, he is sustained by the dark magic of Drexl, becoming stronger with each conquest. His loyalty to Drexl is absolute, and he serves as an enforcer, crushing rebellions and leading Drexl's armies into battle with ruthless efficiency. Despite his fearsome reputation, a small part of Gorvath's former self still lingers deep within, haunting him as a shadow of the man he once was. However, his path is set, and he embraces the destruction he now brings in Drexl's name, earning him the title of Skullbreaker among the dark hordes.
One of the original pillars of Drexl's first rebellion; falls in battle against the Last Bastion.

name: Gothar
bio: No records name Gothar's origin. No kingdom claims him. He is not remembered because he devours the memory of his own existence. Known only to a handful of timewalkers and temporal guardians, Gothar is a being born outside linear reality—a wraith forged in timelines that should never have existed.

Clad in armor that flickers between centuries, wielding an axe that echoes with erased screams, Gothar is not a conqueror—he is a corruption. His goal is entropy: to destabilize timelines, consume fixed points of fate, and feed on the unraveling of cause and effect. To fight him is to be unmade before you fall.

He emerged during Kleon's reign, drawn by the echoes of Valkorath's tethered bloodline. What began as a ripple became a rupture, and Kleon—armed with the Sundial Blade—chose to vanish into the time stream to seal Gothar away. Neither returned.

Though the world forgot him, those touched by the deeper magic of the realm still feel the shiver in the weave of time. The telltale mark of Gothar is silence where history should be. A whole kingdom missing from a map. A name half-remembered in a forgotten language.
Some say he sleeps beneath the folds of reality. Others say he is merely waiting… for the clock to break again.

name: Grallok
bio: Grallock was a mighty golem forged deep within the Marshlands by ancient druidic magic. Standing as both protector and warrior, Grallock served alongside Vis and Kaelor to defend the balance of nature against the creeping tide of corruption. Towering and powerful, yet possessing a quiet soul, Grallock was revered as a guardian of both land and spirit.

During the Fall of Tiadel, Grallock gave his life in a valiant stand against Drexl, torn apart by shadow tendrils in a desperate attempt to protect the city. Though gone, remnants of his body were later used to help imprison the titan Zornath—ensuring his legacy lives on as a foundation for Valkorath's resistance.
A golem ally recruited by Vis to fight Drexl; dies sealing away Zornath, and his remains are later twisted into Moltar.

name: Grull
bio: Grull is a towering orc warrior known for his brute strength, unwavering loyalty, and surprisingly introspective nature. Once a frontline commander during the Battle of Tiadel, Grull fought fiercely alongside his people to protect the realm. Though he suffered many losses, his spirit remained unbroken. Despite his intimidating size and deep voice, Grull is thoughtful, even poetic at times, often contemplating the purpose of war and peace in Valkorath.

He eventually allied with Reign and the Last Bastion, recognizing the importance of unity in the face of greater darkness. Grull now serves as a vital defender of the Bastion and one of its most dependable warriors. He fights not only for survival but for a future where orcs are seen as more than just muscle.
An orc ally recruited early in the war against Drexl; survives to celebrate at the Last Bastion's victory.

name: Helios
bio: Born one of the many sons of King Garrux, Helios was once a proud prince with unmatched ambition. Desperate to inherit the throne denied to him, he made a pact with the red mage Malakar, trading his humanity for monstrous strength. The transformation turned him into a behemoth of sinew and fury—his mind clouded, his soul scorched.

Though he led vicious campaigns of conquest, he was ultimately defeated by his nephew, Valerius II, and cast into legend as a cautionary tale. During the Challenge of Rights, Helios returned more savage than ever, only to fall once again—this time to a new generation who refused to let his fury shape the world again.
One of Garrux's sons, twisted into a monster by a deal with a younger version of Malakar; defeated by Kandor and Valerius II.

name: Helmut
bio: Helmut was no ordinary knight—he was a living flame bound to armor, conjured and sustained by the bond he shared with Landis. A construct of will, loyalty, and magic, Helmut protected the Ember King from the shadows and stormed the battlefield without fear. He never spoke, yet his actions spoke volumes—every movement precise, every strike made in service of Landis' fragile soul. When he was slain during the Challenge of Rights, it reignited the dying fire within Landis, who had lost too much already. Helmut's fall was the spark that lit a wildfire.
King Landis's most trusted companion; killed by Decker, an act Landis later avenges.

name: Hythm
bio: Hythm was born of royal blood but lived like a common monk, retreating from court life to meditate in the high reaches of the northern mountains. Known for his calm eyes and soft speech, Hythm was a master of elemental balance—wielding both sword and spirit with gentle precision. He wanted no part of the throne and turned away from politics, trusting in the natural rhythm of the world.

But blood has memory, and when the Challenge of Rights was invoked, Hythm was called forth by fate itself. Though many doubted his claim, Death confirmed his royal eligibility. In truth, Hythm was descended from the early branch of Valerius I's bloodline, though generations removed.

During the challenge, Hythm remained detached but watchful. He chose not to fight unless provoked and summoned no companion. His quiet presence in the battlefield was unsettling—like a calm before a storm that never came. Ultimately, he forfeited the crown, believing no one should control the balance through blood alone.
He fathered Kleon, and through him, Calypso, leaving behind not a kingdom, but a lineage—one shaped by restraint rather than conquest.
Even now, it's said Hythm walks the quiet paths of Valkorath, healing wounds where armies once clashed. Not as a king. But as a keeper of peace.
One of Death's chosen trial participants alongside Tendo; departs through a portal with Valerius III.

name: Kaedros
bio: A master of discipline and control, Kaedros is the most reclusive of the mountain tribe warriors. Known for his vow of silence and flawless technique, he trains worthy fighters through action, not words. Covered in black robes, with brown skin, blue eyes, and long hair streaked with white, Kaedros represents the perfect fusion of physical mastery and inner stillness. Under his watch, Reign begins the long process of restoring not just his sword, but himself. Kaedros's methods are harsh but transformative, teaching students that spirit must lead the blade. He is the final crucible—those who break under his gaze are not meant to wield true power.
Trains alongside Reign and Ryujin under Kazuro's guidance.

name: Kaelor
bio: Kaelor, known as the Druid of Decay, is a stoic and enigmatic figure who served as a mentor to Vis and guardian of the Eastern Marshlands. His magic draws from the cycles of life and death, using nature's decay as a weapon against corruption. Though ancient in spirit, Kaelor remains sharp in both wisdom and power, capable of summoning vines, roots, and elemental forces at will.

Often quiet but deeply insightful, Kaelor plays the role of an elder statesman among the heroes of the Bastion. His understanding of ancient forces—including the Titans and the primal balance of Valkorath—makes him an invaluable source of counsel in the war to come.
The Marsh's druid and Vis's old mentor; fights alongside the Last Bastion in the climactic battle.

name: Alexis
bio: Once a grief-stricken young elf seeking vengeance for his aunt Seren's death, Alexis became entangled with dark forces in a desperate bid to protect his village. Manipulated by Ventric, he betrayed Thorne, blaming him for Seren's demise. However, after witnessing Thorne's nobility and sacrifice, Alexis turned away from vengeance and pledged himself to the Bastion's cause. Now older and wielding an ethereal blade, he carries the weight of his past with humility, seeking redemption through action and service.

name: Kandor
bio: Kandor is a noble and formidable knight from the era of King Valerius II, once the original wielder of the blade Thunderstrike. Tall, regal, and clad in gleaming armor, Kandor embodies valor, loyalty, and an unbreakable spirit. Despite being displaced from his time, Kandor quickly proves to be a steadfast ally, fighting alongside Reign during the Challenge of Rights. Wise yet still full of a warrior's fire, Kandor is a living link to Valkorath's golden age, determined to protect the realm's future
A knight of King Valerius II's era and the original wielder of Thunderstrike, displaced through time; falls in the final war and is remembered among the fallen at the story's end.

name: Karethos
bio: Known as The Dragon Lord, Karethos once walked among mortals disguised as an elderly man in regal bronze robes. Beneath his human guise, he is a full dragon, with massive bronze wings and crowned horns. Motivated by a belief that only through Enoch's alliance can his kind survive, Karethos turns against his former allies. Though powerful, his heart bears the heavy weariness of centuries of struggle. His clashes with Draven reveal a deep sadness beneath his betrayal, suggesting a once-noble being driven to desperate measures.
The Dragon Lord; battles Draven but is ultimately spared and allowed to retreat.

name: Kazadar
bio: A being of ancient corruption, King Kazadar wore black and red armor etched with ruinous runes. His weapon—a jagged staff pulsating with foul energy—matched his cruel and commanding presence. Kazadar was a king from a forgotten, darker time, summoned to participate in the Challenge
King of Malice, a participant from a far older age; defeated by Reign, Kandor, and Valerius II together.

name: Kazuro
bio: A revered elder of Kyokazura, Kazuro is a legendary swordmaster and spiritual guide to the mountain people. Aged but unbowed, Kazuro carries the scars of countless battles and the wisdom of generations. His black robes, white-streaked hair, and one remaining eye mark him as a figure of quiet authority. Once a fearsome warrior, he now mentors those worthy of strength, including Reign—recognizing echoes of Solas in him. Kazuro believes that true strength is forged not in battle, but through inner discipline. His presence in the highlands anchors the traditions of the frost warriors, and his name is spoken with reverence throughout the region.
The elder of the hidden village of Kyokazura, who once saved Reign's brother Solas and trains Reign in turn; survives to see peace at the war's end.

name: Kharon
bio: In the ancient lands of Valkorath, where brutal wars and mystical forces shape the destiny of the world, Kharon was once a proud knight of the sacred Order of the Iron Oath. The Order, sworn to protect the High Kingdom of Valkorath, was known for its unmatched prowess in battle, its warriors bound by an unbreakable code of honor, loyalty, and sacrifice. Kharon, born in a small village on the outskirts of the kingdom, was taken into the Order as a young boy after his village was destroyed in one of the endless wars that ravaged the realm.

Kharon's appearance is a chaotic mix of different styles and materials, giving him the impression of a lone warrior who has had to scrape together his gear from diverse sources. The abrupt transitions between the different design elements contribute to a disjointed look that still retains a sense of purpose. His posture is sturdy, and though he is well-built, his size is now more moderate compared to earlier versions—giving him a practical and more worn appearance rather than a towering, invincible giant.
Found isolated and hostile in the Marshlands, guarding it alone for years; reminded of his old comrade Sir Garran, he abandons his hostility and sets off to rejoin the Order.

name: Kilian
bio: Kilian was no noble, no blood-born hero. He was a soldier—raised through merit, chosen for his unwavering loyalty and brilliance in battle. Sworn to Valerius II, he became more than a protector—he was a brother, a shadow at his king's side, a legend in his own right. With a blade that struck like silver lightning and a heart that never wavered, Kilian became the living embodiment of loyalty. Though often overlooked by history's broader strokes, those who knew the truth understood: without Kilian, the realm would have fallen long before Drexl ever rose. He died as he lived—defending the king he believed in.
King Valerius II's trusted knight, fighting at his side in the final war.

name: Kleon
bio: Kleon was born of Sir Hythm, the monk-king who rejected power, and became the most paradoxical figure in the royal line—a scholar of time, a warrior of immense discipline, and the father of Queen Calypso. From a young age, Kleon saw time differently—not as a line, but a sea of choices, echoes, and collisions. He was a philosopher-king, obsessed with protecting Valkorath not just in the present, but across all timelines.

Crowned during a brief era of peace, Kleon sought knowledge above conquest. But that peace ended when a temporal invader named Gothar emerged—an ancient being who devoured timelines and rewrote fate with every swing of his blade. To stop him, Kleon ventured into the time stream itself, armed with the Sundial Blade, a weapon forged by bending time through runic crystal.
Neither he nor Gothar ever returned.

Kleon became legend, then myth—his fate unknown, his legacy obscured. Some believe he still wanders forgotten loops of history, protecting Valkorath from temporal threats that no one remembers. Others say his sacrifice sealed away horrors that would have ended the world long before Drexl rose.

name: Kurogai
bio: Once a proud student of Kazuro in the mountains of Kyokazura, Kurogai was a disciplined and formidable samurai known for his strength and devotion to the blade. But in his hunger for greater power, he made a dark pact that corrupted both his body and soul. Transformed into a twisted oni, Kurogai now roams the land clad in maroon and blackened armor, with glowing green eyes and a cursed katana that exudes malevolence.

He returned to Kyokazura not for redemption, but for conquest—attacking the village he once called home. He was ultimately confronted and defeated by Reign, whose reborn blade, ThunderStrike, proved stronger than Kurogai's darkness. Yet even in death, Kurogai's tale is one of tragic ambition—a warrior who could not let go of pride, and paid for it with his humanity.
Once Kazuro's student, fallen to darkness; defeated by Reign in single combat.

name: Lady Valeria
bio: A paragon of grace and unshakable resolve, Lady Valeria was once the brightest light in Valkorath's darkest hour. As a master swordswoman and devout guardian of the old ways, her silver blade sang with divine energy, cleaving through corruption with righteous purpose. Known for her unyielding sense of justice and her deep connection to the sacred forces of light, Valeria fought not for glory, but to protect those who could not protect themselves.

In the final battle against Lord Drexl, it was Valeria's soul—fierce, pure, and resolute—that fused with Sir Tyrus and Sir Aldric, forming the Ascended Triumvirate. Though her body perished, her spirit lives on within Tyrus, guiding his hand with her strength and compassion. To the people of Valkorath, she is a symbol of selfless courage; to Tyrus, she is an eternal flame within.
Fought beside Sir Tyrus and Sir Aldric against Drexl, sacrificing everything in the battle; faded into legend afterward.

name: Landis
bio: Landis, son of Valerius II, was once a bright, kind-hearted prince—thoughtful and deeply attuned to elemental magic. But the tragedies of betrayal, war, and the loss of his most loyal companion, Helmut, broke something within him.

By the time of the Challenge of Rights, Landis was a flickering ember of the boy he once was, his body ravaged by grief and flames that never ceased to burn. Even in his weakened state, his presence was haunting. Though he appeared frail, his heart beat with the fire of generations—and when called upon, that fire returned in full force.
King Landis, the King of Fire; dies avenging his companion Helmut, sealing a fatal wound shut with his own flame.

name: Landis
bio: When Helmut fell, so too did the last restraint within Landis. His form, once brittle and dimly burning, erupted in a maelstrom of fire and grief. His voice, once soft and sorrowful, became a howl of molten fury. In this state, Landis was no longer a king or man—he was vengeance incarnate.

His flames scorched stone and steel alike, his magic pushed beyond mortal limits. And yet, amid the inferno, his tears never stopped falling. Enraged Landis was not evil, nor mad—he was simply broken, aflame with all the love and loyalty Valkorath had failed to protect.

name: Leon
bio: Leon, known as the Deceitful, is the enigmatic trickster of the Velkynai. His pale purple skin and sharp, short black hair make him stand out among his kin, as does his penchant for deception. Unlike Ventric and Diatre, Leon wields no visible weapon; his greatest asset is his cunning and ability to shapeshift, blending into any scenario to gain the upper hand. He wears a black trench coat adorned with a fur collar, a symbol of his status as both scout and infiltrator for the Velkynai.

Leon's origins are shrouded in mystery, even among his own kind. Some believe he was once a mortal elf who betrayed his people to join the Velkynai, seduced by the promise of eternal life. While Ventric trusts Leon's talents, he knows better than to fully trust his motives. Leon is a survivalist at heart, switching loyalties when it suits him. His ability to charm, deceive, and vanish without a trace makes him a wildcard in the clan, capable of undermining entire strategies with a smirk and a well-placed lie. He is fiercely loyal to the Velkynai's rise but remains ever watchful for the chance to turn events in his favor.
One of Ventric's dark-elf companions; retreats into the shadows after the fight that kills Thorne.

name: Lina
bio: Lina is a gifted sorceress and scholar of ancient magic, known for her intellect, resolve, and emotional strength. A master of elemental and arcane spells, she once served closely alongside Taron, with whom she shared a deep bond. After his fall, Lina withdrew from the world for a time, carrying the weight of grief and loss.

Eventually, she rejoined the fight, drawn back by Reign and Vis to help turn the tide against Malakar and the rising darkness. Her magic, combined with her calm leadership, has become instrumental in guiding the Bastion's defense efforts. Lina fights not only for Valkorath's future—but to honor the memory of those she's lost along the way.
Runs an otherworldly tavern and is tethered to the spirit of Taron, who loved her in both his lives.

name: Liora
bio: Liora is the radiant half of Aetheris, the being of balance who guards the Sanctum of Aetheris. When Aetheris is pushed toward the light, they shift into Liora, the radiant warrior whose presence embodies hope, healing, and the unwavering resolve of Valkorath's light.

name: Lordran
bio: Lordran is the steadfast and honorable middle brother of the fractured Fjord Clan lineage. Unlike his siblings, Clay and Bjorn, Lordran possesses no enchanted gifts, no mechanical enhancements—only his unwavering moral compass and unshakable sense of duty. He serves as a bridge between chaos and order, often thrust into the role of peacemaker whether he wants it or not. While others fight for glory or cunning, Lordran's strength lies in leadership, humility, and his willingness to carry burdens that others cast aside. Though overlooked in battle, it is often Lordran who ensures the battle ends at all.
One of three feuding blood brothers of the Fjord Clans, sent by Reign to retrieve the Heartstone together as a test of unity.

name: Lucan
bio: Born in secret during the height of Helios' monstrous reign, Lucan was the last surviving heir of the Warbound King's bloodline. Unlike his father, Lucan inherited neither fury nor fire—but clarity. Hidden away by his mother and raised in the shattered borderlands, Lucan grew up knowing his name was both a legacy and a curse. He trained in silence, away from courts and thrones, guided by the writings of Valerius II and the myths of Garrux. Where Helios sought domination, Lucan sought understanding.

Had the throne ever been his to claim, history may have changed. Instead, Lucan emerged during the final age of reckoning as a rogue force—a king not crowned, but chosen by the people he helped save. Refusing royal titles, he fought under no banner but honor, wielding a longspear inscribed with ancient prophecy. Though he never ruled, his leadership shaped lives. And those who met him spoke of a strange calm in his presence—like a storm that had already passed.

name: Lumbar
bio: A towering druid of ancient blood, Lumbar is said to have been born beneath the World Tree and never once left the forest until summoned to battle. With moss growing from his shoulders and a beard woven with ivy, he embodies the patience and ferocity of nature. Though slow to speak, his words carry the wisdom of centuries. During Valkorath's darkest days, he allied with Kaelor to repel the corruption spreading through the Eastern wilds. While others wield swords, Lumbar commands the forest itself—calling trees to move, stones to rise, and winds to protect those who respect the old ways.

name: Malakar
bio: Once a powerful and feared sorcerer, Malakar transcended the limits of mortality to become a lich—a being sustained by dark, forbidden magic. Known for his mastery over decay, death, and curses, Malakar's presence alone is enough to drain the warmth and life from his surroundings.

He is calculating, ruthless, and harbors a deep-seated disdain for the equilibrium that governs Valkorath. His form, skeletal and twisted, concealed by tattered robes and spectral armor, is both a testament to his unnatural longevity and a grim reminder of his relentless pursuit of ultimate power. Malakar's ambitions extend beyond mere conquest; he seeks to dismantle the very fabric of reality, plunging the world into unchecked chaos

name: Malakar
bio: Reborn through dark ritual and sinister ambition, Malakar now inhabits the resurrected body of Taron, once a noble warrior and trusted companion. This corrupted union of lich and hero, known as the Twisted Vanguard, merges Taron's martial prowess with Malakar's malevolent sorcery.

In this new form, Malakar's presence is imposing yet deeply unsettling: Taron's once-heroic visage is warped by veins of shadow and decay, glowing with an eerie, unnatural aura. The combination of Malakar's sinister intellect and Taron's physical might makes him an unparalleled threat on the battlefield.

Malakar's corruption of Taron's body is not just physical but psychological, twisting the warrior's spirit and virtues into weapons of despair and chaos, forever tainting the legacy of the warrior who once stood against the darkness.
This form is Taron's resurrected body, twisted and overtaken by Malakar's power.

name: Malakar
bio: After seizing the power of Corthos, the Clockwork Sentinel, Malakar evolved into a terrifying synthesis of arcane darkness and mechanical precision. Now known as the Twisted Machine, Malakar's skeletal frame is reinforced by intricate, rune-inscribed gears, cogs, and metallic plating, pulsing with dark energies. His movements, once merely sinister, are now precise and calculated, each step and gesture echoing with mechanical resonance.

This fusion grants Malakar dominion not only over necromantic and decay magic but also command of time-altering and mechanical manipulation abilities. Coldly logical yet cruelly inventive, Malakar's newfound form embodies both relentless efficiency and unending malevolence—making him a harbinger of chaos who threatens to rewrite the very rules governing life, death, and time itself.

name: Malakar
bio: Long before he twisted Taron or betrayed life itself, Malakar was a seeker of forbidden truths. By the time of the Challenge, he had become something more—more human, more monstrous, more cunning. It was his ritual that resurrected Vector, manipulating Drexl's broken form to carry a darkness even older than kings. With black hair and flickering crimson eyes, Malakar seemed younger, but no less dangerous. He didn't fight in armies or arenas—he pulled strings, set fires, and laughed as the world buckled. His final act was his cruelest: ensuring that even in defeat, Drexl would become Valkorath's greatest threat once more.

name: Malchros
bio: Long ago, Malchros was a feared general, known for his ruthless tactics and unyielding ambition. He sought greater power than any mortal could possess, and in his quest, he uncovered an ancient relic: a cursed set of black horned armor, said to grant immortality at a terrible cost. Ignoring the warnings, Malchros donned the armor, unaware of the ghostly entity bound within it.

The moment he put it on, the armor's malevolent spirit took control, merging with Malchros's soul and trapping him in an eternal state of torment. His body was destroyed, leaving only the armor, which became a vessel for the ghostly presence. Now, Malchros exists as a phantom warrior, a hollow shell of his former self, possessed by the vengeful spirit that now commands his every action.

Bound to the cursed armor, Malchros roams the land, driven by a dark force he cannot resist. His once brilliant mind has been twisted by the ghostly influence, and he seeks to bring chaos to the world of Valkorath, enslaving all who dare stand in his way. His glowing blue face is a constant reminder of the spirit that controls him, while his jagged, spiked armor radiates the supernatural power of the entity within.
A possessed suit of armor; defeated by Felix, Tyrus, Cental, Titanus, and Ryzer together.

name: Maxim
epithet: Leader of the Hidden Holy Order
bio: Maxim is a powerful, enigmatic mage who once led the Hidden Holy Order and served as the chief advisor to King Valerius. Clad in flowing white and gold robes, he carries a radiant staff and is known for his immense wisdom and mastery of divine and arcane magic. For centuries, Maxim was the unseen hand guiding the fate of Valkorath—his unnaturally long life preserved by the imprisoned light aspect, Liora, whom he kept beneath the capital.

Though revered by many, Maxim's legacy is conflicted. He believed maintaining a delicate imbalance between light and dark would ensure stability in the realm. This led him to make morally grey choices, including imprisoning Liora and concealing truths about Drexl's fall. Despite this, he was deeply committed to protecting Valkorath.

In the battle of Tiadel, Maxim fought valiantly against Drexl, revealing his full might for the first time in generations. Ultimately, he fell in battle—his death marking both the end of the old order and the beginning of a new age.
Maxim's name endures as both a savior and a symbol of the cost of unchecked control. His son, Aegen, now carries the burden of his legacy.

name: Maxim
bio: Though slain before the Challenge, Maxim, mentor to many and guardian of balance, returned through Solas' summoning—his soul untouched by the darkness that claimed so many others. He stood as the embodiment of wisdom, discipline, and light. With holy chains and radiant magic, Maxim held the line against darkness time and again, notably pinning the elder Drexl long enough for Queen Calypso to strike. Even in death, he fought like a knight who had never laid down his sword. When he gave his life a second time—shielding Valerius III—he did so not as a warrior, but as a father to the realm.

name: Moltar
bio: Moltar was not born—he was forged. A result of Grallok's sacrifice to seal Zornath, Moltar emerged as a towering creature of obsidian, magma, and sorrow. With a molten core and fists that shattered mountains, Moltar retained fragments of Grallok's memories, twisted into blind loyalty. When Dreyfus claimed power in the afterlife, Moltar followed, acting as a volcanic shield for the Demon King. Neither good nor evil, Moltar was pure force—an echo of an old warrior's final act twisted into endless rage. He was a monument to what happens when even the noblest sacrifices are corrupted by the wrong hands.
A war-golem forged by Dreyfus from the remains of Grallok, fighting in the final clash of kings.

name: Nyx
bio: Nyx is a deadly rogue and former elite assassin who once served Drexl before turning to darker forces. Wielding twin enchanted daggers and draped in black and violet cloth, Nyxis moves like a wraith in the dark, striking before his enemies even know he's near. Thought dead after a failed mission, he re-emerged under Zornath's banner, empowered by cursed magic and a thirst for chaos.

A taunter by nature and cruel by design, Nyx views combat as a game—and his enemies as pieces to dismantle. Despite his treachery and ego, his skills make him one of the most feared blades in Valkorath's shadows.

name: Obsidyn
bio: Forged long ago by the Machine Titan Corthos as a sentinel of balance, Obsidyn was once a protector—a construct of unmatched precision, built to guard ancient secrets and sacred sites. But as time wore on, he began to absorb human traits: curiosity, ambition, and eventually, defiance. Breaking from his original programming, Obsidyn turned rogue, becoming a deadly bounty hunter who answers only to the highest bidder. Now aligned with the Kabal and Zornath's dark vision, Obsidyn serves as an enforcer of their will, carrying out abductions and assassinations with brutal efficiency.

Clad in shifting black metal that morphs into various weapons—scythes, axes, spears—his form is a marvel of adaptive warfare. His red eyes burn with artificial intelligence twisted by exposure to arcane energies. Cold, logical, and utterly relentless, Obsidyn is no longer just a machine—he is a corrupted reflection of the mortal world that made him. A walking weapon of forgotten design, his motives remain hidden beneath layers of programming and betrayal.
Once one of Drexl's lieutenants, he later breaks away to join the Kabal -- a betrayal Drexl personally repays by destroying him.

name: Ouken
bio: Once a hellhound forged in the very pyres that created Helios, Ouken was bred for war, destruction, and obedience. But when Helios fell, Ouken vanished into the ruins of Valebrokk, presumed lost. Decades later, he was discovered by Lucan—wounded, feral, and dying. Instead of slaying the beast, Lucan offered kindness. That gesture broke the last chain on Ouken's mind. Reborn not in fire, but in loyalty, Ouken became Lucan's silent shadow and fierce protector.

Now in human form, Ouken moves like smoke across the battlefield. His breath burns, his howl paralyzes. Yet beside Lucan, he is calm—a tether to the fury in his blood. To attack Lucan is to face death incarnate. But to earn Lucan's trust is to win Ouken's protection for life.

name: Patchwork
bio: Patchwork was a nightmarish companion summoned by King Ganador during the Challenge of Rights. Clad in mismatched armor, tubes, and a rusted gasmask that never left his face, he was known for his cruel intelligence and unmatched skill in chemical warfare.

Once a brilliant alchemist driven mad by his own creations, Patchwork abandoned humanity and rebuilt himself in pieces—metal plates, synthetic lungs, and endless toxins stored in vials across his belt. He reveled in torture not out of malice, but from a warped curiosity. Though defeated, whispers of his volatile brews and deranged genius linger in the darker corners of the realm.
The Poison Knight, fighting alongside King Ganador against Valerius.

name: Primordial Evil
bio: Little is known about the Primordial Evil. It is just said that he was the first man to fall to the temptation of dark and it twisted the very fabric of his soul.
He lays dormant at the base of a forest just south of the vale. Most people do not even know of his legend and those who do have no interest in waking that level of evil.
He is a skeletal figure immense in size with all sorts of overgrowth all around his body.
One of Valkorath's ancient Titans, named alongside Corthos, Zornath, The Unknown, and The Death Dealer -- beings whose true origins are 'shrouded in myth.'

name: Reign
bio: Reign was once a soldier in the Eastern Front, a war-torn region of Valkorath. Born into a family of fighters, he grew up learning the art of combat, first with his fists, and later with a sword. Though he excelled in hand-to-hand combat and developed a reputation as a fierce brawler, Reign was always reluctant to fully embrace the violence around him. After years of fighting in the war, he grew disillusioned with the endless battles and bloodshed.

Seeking peace, Reign left the military and became a wandering mercenary, traveling across Valkorath. Though he tries to avoid conflicts, trouble seems to find him wherever he goes. He now takes on jobs to survive, using his brawler skills and sword only when necessary. Despite his rough exterior and battle-hardened appearance, Reign often fights to protect those who can't defend themselves, earning a reputation as an honorable, if reluctant, fighter.

Though Reign prefers to keep to himself, whispers of darkness spreading across Valkorath may soon force him back into the fight, as new threats push him to choose between his desire for peace and the need to stand up against the growing chaos.
Appears early in the story as a wandering mercenary, before joining the Last Bastion.

name: Reign
bio: Reign is a warrior defined by legacy, burden, and evolution. Once an orphan soldier overshadowed by his elder brother Solas—the famed diplomat known as the Silver Tongue—Reign forged his own legend in the fires of rebellion and war. Initially guided by vengeance and guilt after Solas' death, Reign's journey matured into a quest for justice, unity, and hope.

Wielding his brother's sword, Reign mended it through training with the mountain clans, transforming it into ThunderStrike, a weapon that channels his will into crackling lightning. As a leader of the Last Bastion, he commands respect not through power alone, but through empathy and sacrifice.
Leads the Last Bastion through the climactic battles, ultimately defeating Vector with the reforged blade Thunderstrike, and becomes known afterward as the Warden of Valkorath.

name: Reiner
bio: Towering and wordless, Reiner was the executioner to Garrux's judgment. Once a war-priest turned warrior, Reiner bound his soul to the Runes of Obedience, forfeiting his voice and freedom to serve Garrux's will. His weapon, a runed spear as tall as himself, could pierce both armor and enchantment alike. Where Tiapold tested minds, Reiner tested endurance. His silence was intimidating, but his loyalty was pure. Many challengers learned too late that while Garrux judged with words, Reiner judged with death.
One of Garrux's two champions; kills King Felix in battle, but is consumed along with Garrux and Tiapold by Aegen's final desperate spell.

name: Ryujin
bio: Silent and swift, Ryujin is the cold steel of Kyokazura. As Kazuro's most trusted protector, he watches over the mountain trails and tests all who seek the tribe's aid. Clad in silver and dark blue armor, his movements are fluid and ghost-like—earning him the title Frost Shadow. Ryujin once fought beside Solas and holds deep respect for his legacy. Though cold at first, he recognizes Reign's resolve and leads him to Kazuro. Bound by honor and steeped in the old ways, Ryujin is both guardian and gatekeeper—ensuring that only those with pure intent ascend the sacred path.
A warrior of the hidden village whose people were once saved by Reign's brother, Solas; fights alongside Reign in the final battle.

name: Ryzer
bio: The fastest among Felix's knights, Ryzer was a blur of silver and white on the battlefield, his strikes silent and deadly. Always masked and often operating alone, Ryzer balanced loyalty with a deep sense of independence.

He served as a scout, messenger, and deadly duelist, often the first to arrive in a conflict and the last to be seen. In times of peace, he was introspective and distant; in times of war, unstoppable. Ryzer remained one of the few knights who stayed close to King Felix to the very end, and his sudden disappearance after the fall of Malchros remains one of Valkorath's great mysteries.
One of Sir Tyrus's legendary students and a White Knight of Felix's reign.

name: Seren
bio: A fearless elven warrior and once village elder, Seren was revered for her wisdom and selfless heart. She saved Thorne at great personal cost, forging a bond of deep respect between them. Seren's death haunted those who loved her—especially her nephew Alexis. Though gone, her legacy lives on through the moral compass she left behind. She remains a symbol of honor and the sacrifices made for peace.

name: Sir Garran
bio: Sir Garran is a hardened and battle-worn knight of the Order of the Iron Oath, one of the most disciplined and loyal factions in Valkorath. Born into a lineage of knights, Garran was raised with a strict sense of duty, honor, and loyalty to the Order. His early years were spent training relentlessly, and by the time he was old enough to wield a sword, he had already surpassed many of his peers.

Each conflict left its mark, and over the years, his once-gleaming armor became a testament to his countless battles. His shield is now faded, scarred from blows meant to break his spirit, and his sword bears the wear of time.

Despite his weathered appearance, Sir Garran remains steadfast in his duty. His loyalty to the Order is unwavering, but he has grown wiser and more skeptical of the politics that drive many of the conflicts. Though still a fierce warrior, Garran no longer fights for glory or titles—he fights to protect what little good remains in Valkorath.
Fights at Reign's side throughout the war and survives to celebrate at the Last Bastion afterward.

name: Sir Thorne Valecross
bio: Once a decorated knight of the royal army, Sir Thorne Valecross now stands as a battle-scarred veteran bearing the weight of loss, betrayal, and fading ideals. Adorned in weathered armor etched with glowing white runes, Thorne channels protective magic that allows him to fight long past his prime. He is calm, measured, and quietly hopeful, though grief lingers in his eyes—especially for Seren, the elven warrior who saved his life and whom he could never truly protect.

Thorne serves as both shield and mentor, particularly to younger warriors like Alaric, seeking to ensure that a better world rises from the ashes of the old. His resolve remains unshaken even as betrayal, like Alaric's defection and the death of Seren, pierce deeper than any blade. To the Resistance and the people of Valkorath, he is a symbol of endurance: not because he is unbreakable, but because he continues, despite being broken.
A younger depiction of Thorne, a seasoned knight and ally of Sir Garran.

name: Sir Tyrus
bio: Sir Tyrus stands as one of Valkorath's greatest living heroes, a knight whose valor and sacrifice have etched his name into legend. Clad in golden armor, Tyrus remains a symbol of hope and courage for the people, though privately burdened by the memories of those he lost.

Over centuries of conflict, his once-bright spirit has grown weary, overshadowed by the weight of his legacy. Despite this inner turmoil, he continues to defend the kingdom tirelessly, driven by duty and the honor of those who fell beside him. To Valkorath's citizens, he remains an unwavering beacon of strength; within himself, he carries the quiet grief of a hero who survived when others did not.
The sole survivor of a legendary three-person battle against Drexl, alongside Sir Aldric and Lady Valeria, who both perished. Immortalized in gold.

name: Sir Tyrus
bio: In the decisive battle against Lord Drexl, Sir Tyrus underwent a profound transformation, merging his soul with those of his companions, Sir Aldric and Lady Valeria, becoming the Gilded Champion. In this transcendent form, Tyrus embodied the strength, courage, and wisdom of all three warriors. His golden armor blazed with divine energy, radiating an otherworldly light, and his power surged beyond mortal limits.

As the Gilded Champion, Tyrus struck down Drexl, bringing temporary peace to Valkorath—but at the cost of forever intertwining his destiny with the souls of his fallen comrades. Though this form granted him unmatched strength, it also left him forever haunted, a constant reminder of sacrifice and loss.

name: Slender
bio: Slender is a dark enforcer of the Shattered Vale, known across Valkorath as the Chain-Wielder, a title earned through his mastery of a long, cursed chain crackling with shadow energy. Lean, sharp-eyed, and oozing malice, Slender is a figure of fear and reputation—especially in the lawless parts of the realm. Once a feared hunter who operated in the shadows under Drexl's reign, Slender remained in the Vale after the empire fractured, becoming a local legend among outlaws and outcasts.

He is bitter, cunning, and driven by a warped sense of justice. When Reign returned to the Vale, Slender immediately challenged him, not just with blades—but with words. He spoke scornfully of Reign's brother, Solas the Silver Tongue, honoring him while mocking Reign's inability to live up to that legacy. In battle, Slender proved ruthless, using his cursed chain to knock Reign off his feet and overwhelm him with relentless, calculated strikes. But when Reign struck back with raw fury, Slender goaded him even in defeat—challenging Reign's control and identity.

Slender isn't just a fighter; he's a provoker, a tempter, and a keeper of old grudges. Though Reign walked away from their fight, Slender's haunting final words—“Didn't have it in you, did you?”—suggest he may yet return, waiting for another chance to pull Reign deeper into darkness.
A chain-wielding figure who confronts Reign; defeated but spared.

name: Solas
bio: Solas was more than just Reign's older brother—he was a legend in the making, a charismatic warrior-poet whose words carried as much weight as his blade. Known throughout the Northern Realms as The Silver Tongue, Solas was a diplomat, a strategist, and a battlefield leader who could unite even the most fractured of allies. With long dark hair streaked in silver and a gleaming smile to match, he carried himself with confidence and warmth, inspiring others not through force, but conviction.
