Where Reign was quiet and internal, Solas was bold and expressive. He fought not just for victory, but for ideals: peace, justice, and a kingdom built on truth rather than illusion. But his greatest strength was also his burden. Solas understood the cracks in Valkorath's foundation—the manipulations of the Hidden Holy Order, the truths buried beneath Drexl's fall—and he sought to expose them. That ambition made him dangerous to the powers that be.

Solas died under mysterious circumstances, leaving behind his enchanted blade and a legacy that overshadowed Reign for years. His spirit, however, lingers—appearing before Reign in moments of inner conflict. Whether real or imagined, the vision of Solas serves as a mirror: reminding Reign of the light he once followed, and the darkness he now treads. To Reign, Solas is both a ghost and a guide—an unresolved question of whether he is living up to the man he once called brother.

name: Solas
bio: During the Challenge of Rights, Solas returned—not as a ghost or a memory, but as a warrior reborn. Once a fallen hero whose death had marked a turning point in Reign's life, Solas reemerged to fight alongside his brother when the world teetered once more.

With silver-etched armor and his iconic blade, he fought like a man who knew loss intimately but chose love instead of vengeance. Witty and warm, yet devastatingly skilled, Solas was both protector and challenger.
Though he ultimately forwent the throne, his final battle beside Reign reminded Valkorath that heroes are forged in sacrifice, not blood.
Reign's brother; fights at his side as a spirit in the final battle against Vector.

name: Taron
bio: Resurrected by Lina's powerful magic, Taron walked the realm of the living as a spectral warrior, his glowing blue skin and pulsing runes serving as constant reminders of his ethereal state. His long straight white hair, coupled with his blue and black armor adorned with white fur around the collar, gives him an imposing and otherworldly appearance. The blue spirit eagle that accompanies him is not just a symbol of his rebirth but an extension of his spectral might, aiding him in battle.

Taron was a noble spirit warrior with a deeply rooted sense of duty and sacrifice. Once Lina's steadfast companion and a beacon of hope among the heroes, he was known for his honorable heart and spectral combat abilities, granted to him through ancient rituals. He fell many moons ago, only to be later corrupted by Malakar and turned into a grim avatar of darkness.

Even in corruption, a fragment of Taron's spirit remained, which ultimately allowed him to break free momentarily—long enough to inspire those who still fought for good. His legacy lingers in Lina's heart and in the motivations of the Last Bastion's warriors. Taron is remembered as a symbol of valor and tragic loss.

name: Tellan
bio: With wild red hair and eyes burning with intensity, Tellan emerged years into the future as a fierce resistance fighter against the Hidden Holy Order. Known for his impulsive nature and savage skill in battle, he was a key member of Fredric's alliance. Though relatively unknown to Reign at first, Tellan's loyalty and raw power made him a formidable ally. Beneath his brash exterior lies a deep devotion to justice and a personal code forged in the crucible of Valkorath's darkest days.
Introduced briefly alongside a grown Alexis in a gathering of allies; little else about him is established in the story so far.

name: Tendo
bio: Tendo is a mysterious and defiant figure bound to the royal bloodline of Valkorath through distant generations. His most striking feature is his living cloak—an organic, sentient companion that responds to his will. Despite his lighthearted demeanor, Tendo is a capable warrior, having survived countless trials and bearing an understanding of both life and death. When summoned for the Challenge of Rights, Tendo reveals both cleverness and a reckless bravado, making him an unpredictable force on the battlefield
One of Death's chosen trial participants alongside Hythm; dies, and his sentient cloak is later claimed by King Ganador.

name: The Death Dealer
bio: Death is a towering, skeletal figure clad in tattered armor, draped with black wings, and carrying a massive scythe. His voice is hollow yet thunderous, embodying the finality of existence. Far from being just a reaper of souls, Death serves as an ancient arbiter overseeing pivotal events such as the Challenge of Rights—a royal bloodline trial that binds Valkorath's fate. He is sardonic, almost amused by mortal struggles, speaking with an ancient authority that makes it clear: in the end, all living things answer to him
One of Valkorath's ancient Titans, named alongside Corthos, Zornath, The Unknown, and the Primordial Evil.

name: The Unknown
bio: The Unknown is not a warrior, nor a king, nor a creature of clear intent. He is skeletal in form, with hollow eyes that seem to look through time itself. Appearing in moments of great cosmic imbalance--never interfering, never speaking--The Unknown exists outside the known laws of Valkorath. Some claim he is a remnant of a fallen deity, others believe he is the embodiment of consequence itself.

Though he never raises a blade, his presence is felt as deeply as any spell. When he appears, the air chills, and fate itself seems to hold its breath. Drexl feared him. Death acknowledged him. And even the timewalker dared not challenge him.
His purpose remains a mystery. But to those who have seen him and lived--there is no forgetting the feeling that he is not observing you... but remembering you.

name: Thorne
bio: In his younger years, Thorne Valecross was a proud and idealistic soldier—sharp in both appearance and belief. During his time journeying with Seren, an elven warrior of fierce independence, he began to question the rigid systems he once swore by. Their time together was filled with conflict and camaraderie; where he followed order, she followed instinct. That contrast opened his eyes and heart.

He was a capable swordsman even then, but lacked the magical runes that would later define him. Instead, he relied on sheer grit and swordplay. Thorne's bond with Seren became something deeper—unspoken, unresolved, but profound. Her death in battle, sacrificing herself to save him, became the turning point in his life. He left the royal army, carrying her memory and his regret into every battle since.
Though younger Thorne burned with righteousness, it was through loss that he would forge the tempered steel of the man he became.
A noble warrior and mentor figure, betrayed and struck down by his own former student, Frenry.

name: Tiapold
bio: Tiapold, one of Garrux's chosen companions, was once a high war-mage whose mastery of illusion bordered on divine. Though ancient, his mind was sharp, his perception unparalleled. He could conjure realities, disorient armies, and unravel a man's sense of self with a whisper.

Loyal to Garrux not out of fear, but respect, Tiapold believed deeply in merit and truth. During the Challenge of Rights, he served as a veil between challenger and crown, ensuring that only those of true heart and clarity would pass. Beneath his ethereal presence lay a calculating mind—one that had seen generations rise and fall and still remained unmoved.
One of Garrux's two champions; consumed along with Garrux and Reiner by Aegen's final desperate spell.

name: Titanus
bio: Towering and unyielding, Sir Titanus was the shield of the realm and one of King Felix's most stalwart defenders. His massive, battle-scarred armor and slow, deliberate strikes masked a tactical mind and unwavering loyalty. Known for his enduring presence in battle, Titanus was revered for never backing down, even when the odds were insurmountable. During the battle with Malchros, his raw strength was key in buying time for reinforcements to arrive. Though few know where he disappeared to after the war, tales of a towering knight standing vigil in the wilds still circulate among the common folk.
One of Sir Tyrus's legendary students and a White Knight of Felix's reign.

name: Tyrus
bio: Once the beacon of hope for Valkorath, Sir Tyrus was a living legend clad in radiant golden armor, remembered for defeating Drexl during his first rise. But when Drexl returned, more powerful than ever, Tyrus knew he could not sit idly by. Despite his age and the fading strength of his immortal gift, he rode out alone—unwavering in duty, bound by honor.

In his final stand, Tyrus confronted Drexl in the heart of the capital, drawing upon the last remnants of his soul-bound power—shared with the fallen Aldric and Valeria. Though his body was weary, his spirit burned bright. He fought not to win, but to remind Valkorath what courage looked like in the face of overwhelming darkness.

But Drexl was no longer the enemy Tyrus had once defeated. With terrifying ease, Drexl shattered the legend—breaking Tyrus' armor and tossing his body before the people of Valkorath as a warning. Even in defeat, Tyrus' sacrifice stirred something deeper in the realm—a call to arms, a whisper that heroes do not die in vain.

name: Umbros
bio: Umbros is the shadowed half of Aetheris, the being of balance who guards the Sanctum of Aetheris. When Aetheris is pushed toward darkness, they shift into Umbros, the shadowy reaper whose presence embodies the inevitability of decay and the consuming pull of Valkorath's shadow.

name: Valerius
bio: Valerius the First was more than a ruler—he was the bedrock upon which all of Valkorath was built. It was his blood pact with the realm itself that established the magical tether between royalty and balance.
When he appeared to Reign during the Challenge of Rights, he was no longer a man, but a half-dead specter, a flicker of power bound between life and legacy.

With a voice like wind through old halls and eyes that had seen centuries, he chose Reign to inherit not the throne—but the truth. His final act was to pass his power on, declaring the era of kings over, and the age of guardians just beginning.

name: Valerius II
bio: Valerius II was a monarch of vision, compassion, and unmatched martial skill. Grandson of Garrux, he inherited a fractured realm and rebuilt it through diplomacy, strength, and justice. Though his reign was marked by wars and internal strife, Valerius II never strayed from his belief in unity and sacrifice. He led armies from the front, always at his people's side. His friendship with Kilian, his sworn companion, was the stuff of legend. Together, they quelled the rage of Helios and guided Valkorath through one of its most defining eras. He returned during the Challenge of Rights to stand against darkness once more—only to fall with dignity, blade in hand.
Returns from death to fight in the final war alongside Reign and Kandor.

name: Valerius III
bio: Crowned in an age of peace, King Valerius III was groomed from birth to embody wisdom, restraint, and legacy. Raised under the tutelage of Maxim and the Hidden Holy Order, he was taught the old traditions, the sacred laws, and the burden of divine right. For a time, he ruled with clarity and grace, adored by his people and praised as a beacon of stability.

But over the years, his focus shifted—from service to self. Isolated by his upbringing and haunted by the weight of history, Valerius became obsessed with how he would be remembered. His rule drifted toward vanity: grand monuments, portraits, and declarations of greatness that masked his growing detachment from the realm's needs.

As darkness rose in the form of Drexl and Malakar, Valerius found himself ill-equipped for war, retreating further into ceremonial leadership while others—like Maxim and the Resistance—fought to hold the line. Though not cruel, Valerius became symbolic of a deeper flaw in the monarchy: the belief that bloodline alone could guide a kingdom. In the eyes of history, he is not hated, but pitied—a king more concerned with legacy than leadership.
Survives the war and, with the crown's burden lifted, helps usher in a Valkorath without kings or thrones.

name: Vector
bio: VALKORATH Before kingdoms rose and fell, before Drexl's descent into darkness, there was Vector—the firstborn of the original bloodline, hidden from history and sealed away for crimes too great to record. A being of immense magical and divine potential, Vector was consumed by ambition and cast into the Void. It was there, across centuries, that he festered, becoming more myth than man. When Drexl fell, it was through Malakar's final act of sorcery that Vector returned—fused with shadow and wrath, reborn as a living storm. In this avatar form, he was unbound by mortality, an ancient evil seeking not just power, but reclamation of a throne that no longer existed. His defeat marked the final severing of Valkorath's cursed past.
The firstborn of Valkorath's original bloodline, sealed away for ancient crimes; returns through Malakar's sorcery as the final avatar of darkness, and is destroyed by Reign in the climactic battle.

name: Ventric
bio: Lord Ventric is the regal and formidable leader of the elven vampire species known as the Velkynai, a race long hidden from the world, said to descend from ancient elves who dabbled in dark blood magic. Ages ago, during the reign of King Felix, Ventric and his clan were sealed away in the Shadow Crypts, a forbidden realm that held those too dangerous for the world of the living. Ventric's power stems from his staff, which holds a red stone imbued with the life force of countless beings, granting him control over blood and shadow magic. With his long black hair and sharp beard, Ventric has an air of calculated elegance and unyielding command.

Unlike Diatre, Ventric does not hate all living things; he is more complex, driven by an insatiable desire for dominion. He seeks to restore the Velkynai to their rightful place as rulers, believing that their kind is the pinnacle of evolution. Intelligent and strategic, Ventric is a master manipulator who sees battles as games of power, preferring to subjugate rather than destroy. His awakening by King Valerius marks the beginning of his dark ambition to carve out a new era for his people in Valkorath.
Lord of the Velkynai, leading Diatre and Leon; remains a threat the Last Bastion is still working to stop.

name: Vis
bio: Vis was born in the shadowy outskirts of the Eastern Marshlands, a region known for its toxic swamps and deadly creatures. From a young age, she was fascinated by the flora and fauna that thrived in such harsh conditions. While others feared the marsh's venomous plants and creatures, Vis saw them as a source of power and began studying their properties.

Her natural talent for alchemy and magic led her to specialize in poison-based spells and potions, mastering the art of paralysis, sleep, and toxins. Despite her dangerous abilities, Vis is kind-hearted, known for healing the sick and defending the weak. She uses her powers only against those who threaten the peace of Valkorath, offering a gentle hand to those in need. Her poison-coated dagger, though lethal, is rarely drawn, and her spells are more often used to disable rather than destroy.

Vis's closest companion is her snake familiar, Venin, a creature she rescued from the deadly swamps. Venin shares a magical bond with her, often assisting in her spells or providing protection. Many fear Vis due to her appearance and her association with poison, but those who know her understand that her heart is pure, even if her powers are not.

name: Vis
bio: Vis is a cunning and fiercely loyal warrior hailing from the mystic Eastern Marshlands. Once a rogue assassin and master of venomous magic, she grew into a formidable leader after the fall of her homeland. Initially guarded and driven by survival, Vis found a sense of purpose among the heroes of the Last Bastion, forging deep bonds with Reign and the others who fought for Valkorath's future.

With her enchanted serpent companion, Venin, and mastery over trap-based and poison-infused spells, Vis became one of the Bastion's deadliest and most tactical fighters. Her abilities reached a terrifying peak during the battle in the King's Chamber, when she unleashed the Histroph Devourer Spell, transforming Venin into a colossal, magical serpent of destruction.

Haunted by loss—especially the death of her comrade Taron—and hardened by war, Vis balances her sharp pragmatism with deep emotional intuition. As the war against chaos escalates, she stands not just as a warrior, but as a symbol of adaptability, vengeance, and unexpected grace.
Fights at Reign's side through the war's end and stays with him afterward, raising their son Fredric.

name: Vorath
bio: Once known as Vorthar, the Crimson Lancer and a fearsome lieutenant under Lord Drexl, Vorath is the grotesque remnant of what remained after Malakar's dark corruption. Twisted beyond recognition, his flesh is now a writhing mass of sinew and exposed muscle, his jaw barely clinging to his face, and his eyes empty voids of hatred. The elegant lancer who once bent reality with precision strikes has become a monstrous brute of rage and agony—driven not by strategy, but by pain.

His crimson armor lies in fragments, fused into his mutated form like broken memories of the warrior he once was. Tendrils sprout from his back and limbs, moving with unnatural speed and crushing power, capable of impaling or constricting entire battalions. Though his speech is fragmented, moments of lucidity reveal the tortured soul trapped within—haunted by the memories of his loyalty to Drexl and the curse that claimed his body and mind.
Neither fully man nor beast, Vorath is now a living engine of destruction. His existence is a warning to all who dare make pacts with darker powers—because some victories cost more than death.
Reborn in Malakar's image as 'the Torturer'; a more advanced version of the experiment that produced Cytax.

name: Vorath
bio: When Vorath consumed the soul of Priest David, something unexpected occurred. David's pure essence, infused with light and holy magic, began to alter Vorath's form. His hulking, sinewy body became slightly slimmer, and a strange purple hue overtook his once crimson flesh.

The dark magic inside Vorath reacted to David's soul, mutating him further, causing tendrils to sprout from his back, capable of extending and retracting at will. These new tendrils became deadly weapons in battle, but when not engaged in combat, they retreat into his body.

After the transformation, Vorath's once insatiable thirst for torture seemed to fade. Outside of battle, he appears lost and sad, as if David's essence had tempered his violent nature. His demented grin is now gone, replaced with a hollow expression, as if struggling between the darkness that created him and the light that now lingers within him.

name: Vornath
bio: Born from the earliest scars in the world's creation, Zornath was not simply a creature—but a hunger given shape. Towering above cities, composed of molten sinew and writhing mouths, Zornath's very presence twisted the land into scorched ruin. He was drawn forth by the unraveling of balance and nearly consumed Valkorath during the final arc of Drexl's rise.

Contained only through the combined strength of the heroes and the Staff of Containment, Zornath's fall marked the turning point in the war. Yet some believe the Maw still whispers in the deep, waiting for weakness… or a summoner bold enough to call it back.

name: Vorthar
bio: Once one of Drexl's most formidable and loyal lieutenants, Vorthar was a brutal warrior-magister who wielded corrupted elemental lance with devastating precision. Towering in stature and clad in armor scorched by countless battles, he led Drexl's vanguard during the early campaigns to unify Valkorath through fear and strength.
Vorthar's downfall came at the hands of Malakar, who ambushed and overwhelmed him during an early encounter.

name: Zarok
bio: Zarok is Grull's cunning and sharp-tongued companion—small in stature but large in personality. A goblin with a mind for strategy and sabotage, Zarok rides on Grull's shoulder in battle, often serving as both a scout and advisor. His wit and sarcasm are only matched by his keen intellect and surprising battlefield creativity.

Once a misfit with no place to call home, Zarok found purpose alongside Grull and later, with the heroes of the Last Bastion. Though he rarely shows it, Zarok is fiercely loyal to those he considers friends and is often the first to volunteer for dangerous reconnaissance missions. His dynamic with Grull provides both humor and heart, making the duo one of the most memorable alliances in Valkorath.
An orc ally recruited early in the war against Drexl; survives to celebrate at the Last Bastion's victory.

name: Zephyr
bio: Zephyr is a wind monk and the former leader of the Royal Guard, known across Valkorath for his unmatched agility, wisdom, and calm demeanor. Raised in the monastery of Galehaven, he mastered the sacred art of wind channeling, allowing him to move with blinding speed and strike with precision. Though soft-spoken, Zephyr's words carry weight, and his presence commands respect on and off the battlefield.

A stalwart ally of Reign and Aegen, Zephyr has served with unwavering loyalty, even stepping into more active command roles when the kingdom's leadership faltered. Despite his advancing years in the future timeline, he remains a powerful force—physically diminished but spiritually indomitable.
An old friend of Maxim's recruited to train the Last Bastion's forces; fights in the climactic battle.

name: Zornath
bio: Born from the earliest scars in the world's creation, Zornath was not simply a creature—but a hunger given shape. Towering above cities, composed of molten sinew and writhing mouths, Zornath's very presence twisted the land into scorched ruin. He was drawn forth by the unraveling of balance and nearly consumed Valkorath during the final arc of Drexl's rise. Contained only through the combined strength of the heroes and the Staff of Containment, Zornath's fall marked the turning point in the war. Yet some believe the Maw still whispers in the deep, waiting for weakness… or a summoner bold enough to call it back.
A Titan-tier threat known as the Black Kabal; ultimately overwhelmed and sealed away in a stone tomb rather than destroyed outright.
